title: Python generated CLI completion promotion

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Promote Python #892 completion only when the clarified finish line is proven: generated Python owns parser/help/dispatch and codegen-owned primitive executors own generic deterministic file/data/schema/template/output behavior; remaining module-local runtime code is explicitly limited to package-specific judgment, live inspection, mutation orchestration, or thin target adapters.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep this branch Python-scoped. Do not advance TypeScript, Bash, PowerShell, or broad transport work. Do not claim full-generated-cli-complete until static proof can reject generic deterministic behavior hidden in accepted hand-owned runtime entries.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Tighten inventory semantics, migrate bounded representative generic operations to codegen-owned primitives, update proof gates, and route residual package-specific runtime judgment explicitly.
canonical_core.escalate_when: Completion requires inventing a broad DSL, changing non-Python target architecture, or moving live package judgment into generated code.
canonical_core.next_action: Make the Python operation execution inventory and generated-package proof distinguish codegen-owned generic primitive execution from accepted package-specific judgment, then promote current_state only if the proof honestly satisfies #892.
proof_expectations: uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json
completion_criteria: Generated package static proof, Python Docker conformance, root CLI authority, closeout trust, package planning summary, and codegen-owned primitive executor conformance all agree that original Python generated-CLI intent is satisfied.

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: Keep scope bounded to the promoted TODO item and its stated touched paths.
intent.latitude: Bounded decomposition, touched-path narrowing, validation tightening, and plan-local residue routing.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a better-looking fix changes the requested outcome, owned surface, time horizon, or meaningful validation story.

execution.next_step: Make the Python operation execution inventory and generated-package proof distinguish codegen-owned generic primitive execution from accepted package-specific judgment, then promote current_state only if the proof honestly satisfies #892.
execution.proof: uv run python packages/command-generation/tests/primitive_conformance.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; uv run agentic-workspace doctor --target . --modules planning --format json

invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: prompt.init, prompt.uninstall, and prompt.upgrade now execute generated operation resources through codegen-owned run_operation_steps while workspace runtime supplies module selection and prompt payload compatibility.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: Migrating or reclassifying the remaining generic deterministic runtime-debt operations: delegation-outcome.append and system-intent.sync.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Prompt operations needed an explicit workspace-prompt module IR ownership namespace before representative IR could be accepted by contract tooling.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: Migrate or retire delegation-outcome.append and system-intent.sync, then rerun Python generated-package static, local conformance, Docker conformance, package tests/lint, contract tooling, structured inventory, and primitive conformance.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: Handle delegation-outcome.append and system-intent.sync, then evaluate whether full-generated-cli-complete can be claimed honestly.

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: The command-package IR can promote Python from adapter-layer-proven-not-full-generated-cli to full generated CLI completion only after parser, dispatch, interface, proof, runtime-handler boundaries, and codegen-owned generic primitive execution are all proven.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_run.what happened: Migrated prompt.init, prompt.uninstall, and prompt.upgrade to generated operation resources executed through codegen-owned run_operation_steps; added prompt selection/render handlers to the workspace operation IR executor; added workspace-prompt IR ownership namespace; extended static proof to require prompt adapters to consume generated operation IR.
execution_run.scope touched: Workspace runtime prompt adapter, workspace operation IR executor, prompt operation contracts/registry, operation primitive ownership, Python execution inventory, generated workspace prompt operation resources, generated-package static proof, and active #892 planning surface.
execution_run.validations run: uv run agentic-workspace prompt init --target . --format text; uv run agentic-workspace prompt upgrade --target . --format json; uv run agentic-workspace prompt uninstall --target . --format json; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-docker-conformance --require-docker; uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run pytest tests/test_generated_command_package_proof_runner.py tests/test_contract_tooling.py tests/test_workspace_cli.py tests/test_lifecycle_smoke.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py --quiet-success; uv run python scripts/check/check_structured_file_inventory.py --quiet-success; make test-workspace; make lint-workspace
execution_run.result for continuation: #892 remains open; prompt rendering is no longer generic runtime debt, and remaining generic-deterministic-runtime-debt entries continue to block full completion claims: delegation-outcome.append, system-intent.sync.
execution_run.next step: Migrate or reclassify delegation-outcome.append and system-intent.sync, then evaluate full-generated-cli-complete.

intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: Prompt rendering moved out of generic deterministic runtime debt, but full Python generated-CLI completion remains blocked by delegation-outcome.append and system-intent.sync.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: #967: promote-to-plan reintroduced schema-invalid decomposition metadata; encountered again while promoting the completion lane.
#968: operation input contract advertised memory.route-report --verbose, but the generated parser rejected it.

continuation: Migrate remaining generic-deterministic-runtime-debt entries from python_operation_execution_inventory.json into codegen-owned primitive execution before claiming #892 completion: delegation-outcome.append, system-intent.sync.
